name: Stepper
description: A stepper guides an operator through a task in ordered steps — onboarding a new vehicle, building a report, running a bulk assignment. It shows progress and what's left to do.
keyRule: Use a stepper for a sequence that benefits from being broken up and tracked. For peer views with no order, use tabs instead.

variants.name: Horizontal
variants.whenToUse: A few steps with short labels; fits across the top.

variants.name: Vertical
variants.whenToUse: Steps with more content; content expands under each.

variants.name: Linear
variants.whenToUse: Steps must be completed in order.

variants.name: Non-linear
variants.whenToUse: Operators may jump between steps freely.

anatomy.name: Step content
anatomy.description: the fields/actions for the active step.

behaviors.name: Progress
behaviors.description: Completed steps show a check; the active step is highlighted; upcoming steps are muted.

behaviors.name: Validation
behaviors.description: In a linear stepper, a step must be valid before Next; show errors on the step that needs attention.

behaviors.name: Back/skip
behaviors.description: Allow going back; mark optional steps as skippable where appropriate.

states.treatment: Primary circle with the step number; bold label.
Error-colour indicator on the affected step.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Horizontal fits a few short-labelled steps across the top; vertical expands content under each step.

props.name: linear
props.description: Linear = steps must be completed in order. Non-linear = operators may jump between steps freely.

props.name: steps
props.description: The ordered list of steps with label, optional subLabel, and optional state override.

props.name: activeStep
props.description: Zero-based index of the current step.

props.name: onStepClick
props.description: Called when a reachable step is clicked (non-linear, or a completed/current step in a linear flow).

doThis: Keep steps few and clearly named.
Validate before advancing in a linear flow.
dontDoThis: Don't use a stepper for unordered, peer views.
Don't bury errors — surface them on the relevant step.
Don't create long, dozen-step flows; split the task.
accessibility: Convey the current step and total ("Step 2 of 3") to assistive tech; the active step uses aria-current="step".
Steps are keyboard reachable (where navigable) with visible focus.
Errors are announced on their step.
